commonIssues:
Many beneficiaries, particularly the elderly and manual labourers (e.g., in construction or agriculture), struggle to get their fingerprints verified, as the system often fails to recognize them.

How to fix:

  • Request the FPS dealer to use the 'Nominee' facility. You can add a family member as a nominee who can authenticate on your behalf.
  • Visit a TNEPDS center or Tahsildar office to update your biometrics or request for an OTP-based authentication if fingerprints consistently fail.

A large number of teenagers and adults face potential suspension of ration services if their e-KYC is not completed or if the data doesn't match Aadhaar records.

How to fix:

  • Use the TNEPDS mobile app or portal (tnpds.gov.in) to check your Aadhaar linking status.
  • If not linked, upload the Aadhaar copy through the portal or visit the nearest FPS with a physical copy of the Aadhaar card for seeding.

While aimed at reducing corruption, the online portal for applying for new, corrected, or updated smart cards often shows technical glitches.

How to fix:

  • Try accessing the portal during non-peak hours (late night or early morning).
  • If the portal is unresponsive, visit a local E-Seva center where staff have dedicated access lines to process applications.

In 2025, over a lakh applicants faced delays in receiving new smart cards due to administrative reasons, which affected their ability to enroll in state welfare schemes like the Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thogai Thittam (KMUT).

How to fix:

  • Track your application status on tnpds.gov.in. If approved but card not received, you can download a 'Digital Ration Card' from the portal which is legally valid for all welfare schemes.
  • Contact the District Supply Officer (DSO) if the application is pending for more than 45 days.

Updating an address is difficult, with reports of officials at special camps refusing to update addresses for transfers between districts, or requiring only gas bills despite other documents being valid.

How to fix:

  • Apply for address change online through the 'Smart Card Services' section on the TNEPDS portal. Upload valid proofs like Aadhaar, Voter ID, or Rental Agreement.
  • If rejected at local camps, visit the Civil Supplies and Consumer Protection Department office at the Taluk level with all original documents for manual verification.

Complaints include delays in opening shops, poor quality of food commodities, and dealers asking to keep cards (which is prohibited).

How to fix:

  • Never leave your Smart Card with the dealer. It is your property and required for every transaction.
  • Report any malpractice, poor quality, or irregular timings to the toll-free helpline 1967 or 1800-425-5901. You can also use the TNEPDS app to register a complaint.
